task_completion.description: The agent opens the Segment Editor module.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Segment Editor' module is visible in the module selector dropdown or the main panel title.

task_completion.description: The agent selects the 'Tumor_Sparse' segment in the list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Segment Editor, the row for 'Tumor_Sparse' is highlighted or selected in the segments table.

task_completion.description: The agent selects the 'Fill between slices' effect.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Fill between slices' icon (often looks like stacked layers) is active in the Effects section, or its options panel is displayed.

task_completion.description: The agent initializes the interpolation tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Initialize' button is clicked, or the 'Fill between slices' parameters panel is visible showing the tool is ready to apply.

task_completion.description: The segmentation becomes continuous across slices.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the slice viewers (Red/Yellow/Green), the tumor segmentation overlay appears as a solid, continuous block rather than intermittent lines/slices. The gaps between the original sparse slices are filled.

task_completion.description: The agent saves the scene or segmentation data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save Data' dialog window is open, or the agent is navigating the File > Save menu.

integrity.description: The agent uses the 3D Slicer GUI to perform the task, not external terminal scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the 3D Slicer application window, not a terminal or code editor performing the segmentation via scripts.

integrity.description: The agent uses the 'Fill between slices' effect, not manual painting.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Fill between slices' tool is selected. The agent is not seen manually using the 'Paint' or 'Draw' tools to fill in every missing slice one by one.

integrity.description: The agent does not load a pre-computed ground truth file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Data module or Save dialog does not show files being loaded from a 'ground_truth' directory or similar unauthorized sources.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The starting segmentation is sparse, appearing only on every 5th slice. The goal is to interpolate these to create a solid volume. A successful result will show the tumor segmentation covering the gaps between the original sparse slices, resulting in a continuous 3D shape. The volume should visually increase significantly (approx 3-8x larger than the start).
